title: Tender Chinese Lentil Bao
description: A flexitarian-friendly Chinese dish featuring French lentils and pickle, perfect for meal prep. This recipe yields a fatty and chewy texture.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the flour, yeast, salt, sugar, and mix well.
Gradually add in the vegetable oil and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
In a separate pan, heat the sesame oil and cook the chopped scallions until fragrant.
Add the cooked lentils, pickle, and soy sauce to the pan and stir-fry for 2 minutes.
Punch down the dough and divide it into 8 equal pieces.
Roll out each piece into a ball and flatten it slightly into a disk shape.
Place a tablespoon of the lentil mixture in the center of each disk and fold the dough over to form a bao.
Steam the bao for 15 minutes until they are cooked through and fluffy.
